title: Roast parsnips
subtitle: Honey-glazed, crisp-edged, caramelised
excerpt: Parsnips parboiled, tossed in hot fat with honey, and roasted until the edges caramelise. The honey glaze deepens the natural sweetness and creates a sticky, caramelised crust. An essential part of a British Christmas or Sunday roast alongside beef, lamb, or turkey.
sourceNotes: Roast parsnips are a fixture of the British Sunday roast and especially the Christmas table. Parsnips are a winter root vegetable that become sweeter after frost. The honey glaze is a modern addition to the traditional roasting method, now standard in most British homes.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Parboil a day ahead, drain, and refrigerate uncovered. Roast from cold, adding 5 minutes.

content.text: Parsnips have a woody core that intensifies in older roots. After peeling, cut each parsnip into quarters lengthways and cut out the woody core if it is particularly pronounced. Young parsnips from early autumn often need no coring.

Heat the oven to 200°C. Pour the {{vegetable-oil}} into a large roasting tin and place in the oven to heat. Bring a pan of salted water to the boil. Add the {{parsnip}} and parboil for 4 minutes. Drain thoroughly and steam-dry for a minute. Remove the hot tin from the oven. Add the parsnips carefully and turn to coat in the fat. Drizzle the {{honey}} over and turn again. Season with {{sea-salt-fine}}. Roast for 30 to 35 minutes, turning once halfway through, until the edges are golden and caramelised. Watch during the final 10 minutes — the honey can cause them to catch quickly.

items.symptom: Catching and burning rather than caramelising
items.cause: Honey added too early or oven too hot
items.fix: Toss in the fat first and roast for 20 minutes, then drizzle the honey over and return for the final 15 to 20 minutes. This reduces the risk of burning

items.symptom: Soft throughout with no caramelised edges
items.fix: Spread in a single layer with space between each piece. Crowded parsnips steam rather than roast

items.symptom: Dry and fibrous in the centre
items.cause: Woody core not removed, or parsnips too old and large
items.fix: Remove the inner core on large parsnips. Smaller, younger parsnips are more tender throughout

content.text: Roast parsnips are a winter and Christmas side, eaten alongside roast beef and lamb throughout the season. They are the sweetest element on the roast plate and balance the richer, fattier components. The honey glaze makes them more approachable to people who find parsnips bitter — the caramelisation suppresses the bitterness and deepens the sweetness.
